description: Matrix-free order-2 measurable SDP (Phase-1) on the Ambrus X_23 config, k=4. Verdict: FEASIBLE => order-2 does NOT certify chi_m >= 5 at k=4.
solver: e3u linearized-ADMM, matrix-free (e3s operator), OSQP-direct x-update

run.stopped: manually killed at iter 5120; trajectory decisive, formal prim<1e-3 threshold ~6h of ADMM tail away with no informational value

interpretation: margin (min Bochner slack) fell 1.24 -> 0.0021 (600x) monotonically with NO plateau, and the primal residual tracked to the ~1e-3 floor; this is the feasible signature (contrast the certifying plateau of Moser k=3 at prim 0.278). So order-2 (PSD + IEC up to subset size 4) does NOT certify chi_m >= 5 at k=4. By color-monotonicity (a k-feasible order-2 moment solution zero-pads to a (k+1)-feasible one; S_k-symmetrization is lossless), k=5 is also feasible, so order-2 cannot certify chi_m >= 6 either. The order-2 measurable SDP route is closed; redirect to order-3 (IEC size 6).
caveats: First-order (linearized-ADMM) verdict, validated 3/3 against e3q's dense margins on small configs (rhombus, Moser); no independent X_23-scale cross-check exists because the dense affine map is the 195 GiB wall this solver was built to avoid. 'Feasible' is the absence of a certificate (the negative outcome), so there is no certificate object to externally verify.
